Curated Chaos · Hero Intro · Full Storyboard

Fragments of Real People

A scroll-driven title sequence: he casts, the universe shatters into black glass, and through the fight each of the six fragments surfaces on its own motif — until the sun, the blinder, the black, and the invitation to make your own.

Shots17 Keyframes15 locked + Baver GradeGold & black · grainy analog Continuity@Husam + @HusamHero, face-audited StructureFragments woven through the fight
battle beat fragment reveal shard-wipe (no hard cut) eye-blinder flash → black

“Curated Chaos — Fragments of Real People”

Motif / action
He conjures a single razor-glass disc; composed, gold-and-black, shards just lifting.

The Shatter

“Six were witnessed.”

Motif / action
He casts — the world fractures into black-glass shards exploding outward in 3D depth. The six are loosed into the fight that follows.

Act II

The Fight — fragments woven in

Shots 03–15
03Fragment

Fragment I — Agustín

“A minimalist visual poem about artistic awakening.”

Motif / action
The disc itself — the razor-glass disc as a vinyl record, gold-and-black, turning in the tumbling glass. His weapon coalesces from the shatter.

Action

“He fought to keep them whole.”

Motif / action
He hurls the razor-disc; a hooded attacker lunges; he dodges, mantle flaring.

Fragment II — Najoua

“Kindness mistaken for weakness — until she spoke.”

Motif / action
Lotus / petals — glimpsed fast, reflected in a tumbling shard mid-fight. Never a slow standalone scene (tempo rule).

K11 · 64b1226c

06Battle

Twin Spiral (Q) — charge

“Two discs woke in his hands,”

Motif / action
His Q ability, beat 1 of 2: both discs summoned over open palms, wrapped in the ember-fire sigil — the wind-up right before the throw. Telekinetic, no touching.
Keyframe
K13 · c48ed63d
Transition in
Shard-wipe
07Battle

Twin Spiral (Q) — throw

“and he loosed them, spiralling.”

Motif / action
Beat 2 of 2: the two discs hurled forward, spiralling around each other into one drilling projectile, bursting at the tip. Discs on focus, he set back.
Keyframe
K14 · 98ab701e

Shard-wipe
08Fragment · a battle move

Fragment VI — Federica · Espera

“Foglia d'Oro — the gold leaf beneath her skin.”

Motif / action
A fragment that IS a battle move: the lapis pendant falls from a disc-gate; he lunges to catch it as a second gate behind shows only the chain — teleported back to hand.

K8 · c6a5f493

09Battle

Dodge (mid-fight)

“Every strike, he turned aside —”

Motif / action
Jumps back from a ranged attack, spinning two discs from both hands — his Cheeky Jump.
Keyframe
K6 · 402506da
Transition in
Shard-wipe
10Battle

Spin interrupted — blades destroyed

“— then the blow that broke his discs.”

Motif / action
The enemy's strike interrupts his spin and DESTROYS his two glass discs in a sudden orange blast (momentary diegetic light in the void). He staggers, gritted, soot-marked; disc fragments blow outward. Also usable as a spare standalone clip.
Keyframe
K15 · 33677d48
Transition in
Shard-wipe
11Fragment · a battle move

Fragment III — Başak

“From protective invisibility to deliberate presence.”

Motif / action
A fragment that IS the damage beat: blue evil-eye (nazar) in blue smoke at the golden-ratio focal point as he takes the hit — the last drop that triggers Flame Inside. (User-preferred earlier take.)

K9 · 142f2014

“The fire he carried turned outward.”

Motif / action
The passive ignites from the hit — inner fire visible, discs enhanced, analog embers and smoke (not CG).

Act III

The Close

Shots 16–17
16Battle

Eye-blinder → settle

— no on-screen text —

Motif / action
The sun whites out the whole frame in a white blinder, then the flash FALLS TO REVEAL the settle (K5) — him standing in the dust, embers falling. No pure blackout. (Verified: flash peaks 0.93 → resolves onto K5.)
Keyframe
K5 · c1b64585
Transition in
White flash → reveal K5
17Battle

Call to action

“Dark & fantastical elements. — Create your own fragment →”

Motif / action
CTA over the settle frame (faint scrim for legibility). The button launches the playbook (Phase 0), where the viewer begins their own fragment.
Keyframe
Over K5 settle
Transition in
Fades up over the settle

Fragments are woven through the fight. Instead of a six-block parade before the battle, each fragment surfaces between battle moves so the fight's momentum carries the reveals. Two fragments are themselves battle moves — Federica's pendant-catch (shot 07) and Başak's evil-eye damage (shot 08).

Cadence note. There are four battle beats and six fragments, so the interleave leaves two short fragment-pairs (07–08 the exchange of blows, 10–11 the quiet before the ultimate). A tighter one-move-one-fragment cadence would need two or three more battle keyframes.

These stills are the video's scene frames. Each locked keyframe is the source frame for a hero-video clip (image → video, next phase); the site plays the video scenes, not static images. Tempo rule: delicate motifs are delivered fast — close-ups on the shard-wipes and reflections in the tumbling glass — never slow standalone scenes.

Curated Chaos — 360° continuity of character, object, location
